tagline: Self-serve post-purchase order edits for Shopify

benefits.label: Ticket Deflection
benefits.description: Self-serve order edits. When customers can edit their own orders, they stop emailing support about it.

editTicketRate.label: % of orders that generate an edit-related ticket

reductionRate.label: Ticket reduction from self-serve editing
reductionRate.source: dtcmvp estimate (partner claims up to 98%, Oh Polly apparel case study)

benefits.label: Upsell Revenue
benefits.description: Post-purchase add-ons. The editing flow surfaces upsell items, creating a net-new revenue stream from customers who already bought.

upsellRate.label: % of orders that convert an upsell
upsellRate.source: dtcmvp estimate (partner cites Origin USA $22k/2mo, David Protein ~$1k/day)

upsellPctOfAov.label: Upsell value as % of AOV
upsellPctOfAov.source: dtcmvp estimate (typical add-on is ~40% of original cart)

sources: Oh Polly case study: 98% reduction in order-change enquiries (2,000 to 40/month)
Origin USA: $22,000 upsell revenue in 2 months
David Protein: ~$1,000/day attributed to Order Editing

narrative.headline: Order Editing could add {totalValue}/yr to {brandName} through self-serve order edits.

byDepartment.CX / Support: For {contactName} on the CX team: Order Editing eliminates the most repetitive ticket type (order changes). The {costSavingsTotal}/yr in savings comes from customers fixing their own orders instead of contacting support. Your agents get to focus on real problems.
byDepartment.Ecommerce / DTC: For {contactName} on the ecom side: Order Editing is a dual play. {costSavingsTotal}/yr in ticket deflection plus {revenueTotal}/yr in upsell revenue from the self-serve editing flow. Customers who edit their orders also add items.
byDepartment.Marketing / Growth: For {contactName} and the marketing team: the upsell side of Order Editing adds {revenueTotal}/yr by surfacing add-on products during the order edit flow. This is net-new revenue from customers who already bought.
byDepartment.Founder & CEO: For {contactName}: Order Editing adds {totalValue}/yr to {brandName} across support savings ({costSavingsTotal}/yr) and upsell revenue ({revenueTotal}/yr). At your {targetMultiple} target, the ceiling is {maxMonthly}/mo.
byDepartment.Other: Order Editing adds {totalValue}/yr to {brandName} by letting customers edit their own orders (saving CX time) and surfacing upsell opportunities during the edit flow.

byCategory.Apparel & Fashion: Apparel brands see the highest order-edit rates because of sizing, color, and address changes. {brandName}'s ticket deflection savings are likely above our baseline SWAG. Oh Polly (a fashion brand) saw a 98% reduction in edit-related inquiries.
byCategory.Food & Drink: Food and beverage brands benefit from order edits when customers want to swap flavors, adjust quantities, or add items before shipment. The upsell opportunity is strong because add-on products are natural complements.
byCategory.Other: Order edit rates vary by category. Brands with configurable products (size, color, quantity) see the highest ticket deflection. {brandName}'s actual edit rate depends on product complexity.

notes: Site does not mention return prevention or processing-fee recovery. Those benefits may exist but are not publicly supported. omitted from this SWAG. Pricing not visible on site (hidden behind demo booking).
